blocks.content: Before we axiomatize the real numbers, we need language for "nothing in E exceeds this value."

blocks.latex: M \text{ is an upper bound of } E \subset \mathbb{R} \iff \forall x \in E,\, x \le M

steps.prompt: Drag a point on the number line to explore upper bounds of the set E = (0, 1).
Any upper bound M of (0, 1) must satisfy M ≥ ___

feedback.correct: Correct — 1 is the least upper bound of (0, 1).

incorrect.message: An upper bound must be at least as large as every element of E. What is the smallest value that still bounds (0, 1)?

blocks.content: An upper bound of E is any number ≥ every element of E. The least upper bound (supremum) is the smallest such bound when it exists.
